channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
item.description: A major bug in Oracle's ERP software disproportionately affected American universities, and hackers have capitalized by stealing gobs of data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
item.description: Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the futu...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
item.description:
Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them.
The malware is a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
item.description: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Ameri...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
item.description: A 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability discovered in the phpBB forum software allows an attacker to log in as any user, including administrators. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
item.description:
Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself.
Sygnia, which tracks the group as V...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
item.description: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
item.description: More than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) are distributing a Linux rootkit and infostealer malware targeting credentials and access tokens. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Early Warning Signs of Supply-Chain Attacks Live in the Dark Web
item.description: GitHub access sales, leaked repositories, and stolen API keys can all become supply-chain attack footholds. Flare explores how underground forums expose early signals tied to software supply-chain ris...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
item.description: Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explained....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines.
Called Age...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft fixes Windows update failures linked to WUSA installer
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue that caused Windows updates released since May 2025 to fail when installed via the Windows Update Standalone Installer (WUSA) from a network share. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
item.description: For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
item.description: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk, the world's largest producer of insulin, disclosed a data breach affecting patient information from some clinical trials. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution.
LangGrap...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
item.description:
An INTERPOL-led operation last month resulted in the disruption of Sniper Dz, a decade-long phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform, Group-IB said Thursday.
The effort, codenamed Operation Ramz, took ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered government agencies to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw within three days, as mandated by the newly issued Bindin...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Over 73,000 French govt employees affected in Tchap messenger breach
item.description: The French government revealed that a recent breach of its Tchap encrypted messaging platform affects the accounts of over 73,000 employees in the French public sector. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
item.description:
Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks.
Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling of ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
item.description: Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Japanese energy firm loses drive with data of 10.9 million clients
item.description: Kyushu Electric Power Co., Inc. has disclosed a physical security incident that affects private data of more than 10 million customers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Maine breach portal abused to publish fake data breach disclosures
item.description: In an unusual misinformation campaign, fraudulent data breach disclosures were submitted to Maine's official breach portal and publicly posted before their legitimacy could be verified, prompting comp...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion crew exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft to break into enterprise systems, steal data, and demand payment to keep it private. The campaign hit universities hard...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Oracle mitigates PeopleSoft zero-day exploited in data theft attacks
item.description: Oracle is warning about a critical PeopleSoft Suite zero-day vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-35273 that allows unauthenticated remote code execution, with the flaw actively exploited in ShinyHunter ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
item.description: Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
item.description: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data thro...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
item.description:
Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft Defender.
...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Claims 478 Victims, Can Spread Like a Worm
item.description: A new analysis of The Gentlemen operation has revealed that the financially motivated threat group initially operated as an affiliate responsible for conducting double extortion attacks, while leverag...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Authorities dismantle 'AudiA6' ransomware crypto-laundering service
item.description: Law enforcement has dismantled the “AudiA6” cryptocurrency service allegedly used by ransomware actors and other cybercriminals to launder more than $380 million. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
